# MediaLocker — Full LLM Reference

> MediaLocker is cloud object storage built for media creators. It provides buckets,
> media tagging, categories, sets (variant renditions), storyboards (ordered clip
> sequences), per-organization usage & billing, and short-lived presigned URLs. Object
> bytes always move directly between the client and storage over signed URLs — the
> platform is never a byte proxy. Everything is reachable through three programmable
> surfaces that share one credential and scope model: a REST API, a hosted MCP server
> for AI agents, and the `medialocker` CLI.

This document is the expanded companion to https://docs.medialocker.io/llms.txt. It
inlines enough to actually drive each surface. For canonical detail, follow the links
into https://docs.medialocker.io/.

## Canonical domains

| Service      | URL                                            |
|--------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Docs         | `https://docs.medialocker.io`                  |
| REST API     | `https://api.medialocker.io` (endpoints under `/api`) |
| OpenAPI spec | `https://api.medialocker.io/api/openapi.json`  |
| MCP server   | `https://mcp.medialocker.io/mcp`               |
| MCP llms.txt | `https://mcp.medialocker.io/llms.txt`          |
| Marketing    | `https://medialocker.io`                       |

## Authentication (shared by all three surfaces)

- **Header:** `Authorization: Bearer <secret-access-key>`
- **Credential:** an API key. The public identifier (Access Key ID) has the form
  `ml_<32 hex>`; the secret is a 64-character hex string shown **only once** at creation.
- **Create a key:** in the dashboard under "API Keys", or `POST /api/api-keys`
  (requires `admin` scope), or `medialocker apikeys create <name>` in the CLI.
- **Scopes** (a key may hold any combination):

  | Scope    | Grants                                                            |
  |----------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
  | `read`   | List/read media, buckets, sets, storyboards, usage                |
  | `write`  | Create/update objects, tags, sets; presign uploads                |
  | `delete` | Delete media, buckets, and other resources                        |
  | `admin`  | Everything, including key management and billing (implies others) |

- **Allowlist:** a credential can additionally be narrowed to specific tools and/or
  buckets beyond its scopes.

---

# 1. REST API

Base URL `https://api.medialocker.io`; all endpoints are under `/api`. Send the bearer
header on every request. Responses are JSON.

## Endpoints by area

**Identity & keys**
- `GET /api/me` — current user
- `GET /api/api-keys` — list keys
- `POST /api/api-keys` — create a key (requires `admin`)
- `DELETE /api/api-keys/{id}` — delete a key
- `PUT /api/api-keys/{id}/rotate` — rotate a key's secret

**Buckets**
- `GET /api/buckets` — list
- `POST /api/buckets` — create
- `GET /api/buckets/{id}` — info
- `DELETE /api/buckets/{id}` — delete

**Media**
- `GET /api/media` — list objects (`bucketId`, `kind`, `search`, `sort`, `order`, `limit`, `offset`)
- `GET /api/media/{id}` — metadata
- `DELETE /api/media/{id}` — soft-delete
- `PUT /api/media/{id}/metadata` — set metadata
- `GET /api/media/{id}/thumbnail` — stream derived thumbnail/poster
- `GET /api/media/{id}/stream` — redirect to a presigned GET URL

**Tags**
- `GET /api/tags?search=<q>` — search
- `POST /api/tags` — create
- `DELETE /api/tags/{id}` — delete
- `PUT /api/objects/{id}/tags` — set an object's tags (replaces, not appends)

**Categories**
- `GET /api/categories` — hierarchical tree
- `POST /api/categories` — create
- `DELETE /api/categories/{id}` — delete
- `PUT /api/objects/{id}/categories` — set an object's categories

**Sets** (variant renditions)
- `GET /api/sets` — list
- `POST /api/sets` — create
- `GET /api/sets/{id}` — get
- `DELETE /api/sets/{id}` — delete
- `POST /api/sets/{id}/items` — add item
- `DELETE /api/sets/{id}/items/{itemId}` — remove item
- `POST /api/sets/{id}/generate` — enqueue variant generation (`202 Accepted`)

**Storyboards** (ordered clip sequences)
- `GET /api/storyboards` — list
- `POST /api/storyboards` — create
- `GET /api/storyboards/{id}` — get
- `DELETE /api/storyboards/{id}` — delete
- `POST /api/storyboards/{id}/clips` — add clip
- `PUT /api/storyboards/{id}/clips/reorder` — reorder clips
- `PUT /api/storyboards/{id}/clips/{clipId}` — update clip
- `DELETE /api/storyboards/{id}/clips/{clipId}` — remove clip

**Search**
- `GET /api/search?q=<query>` — full-text search. `q` is **required**; optional
  `kind`, `tags`, `categories`, `bucketId`, `sizeMin`, `sizeMax`, `dateFrom`, `dateTo`,
  `limit`, `offset`. Response includes `facets` (tag/category/kind counts).

**Usage**
- `GET /api/usage` — current snapshot
- `GET /api/usage/history?days=30` — history
- `GET /api/usage/events?days=30&limit=100&offset=0` — events

**Billing**
- `GET /api/billing/subscription` — current subscription
- `POST /api/billing/capacity/add` — add capacity
- `PUT /api/billing/capacity/auto` — configure auto-capacity
- `POST /api/billing/downgrade` — downgrade plan
- `GET /api/billing/invoices` — list invoices
- `GET /api/billing/portal` — Stripe portal URL

**Presign** (direct-to-storage)
- `POST /api/presign/upload` — single-shot upload presign
- `POST /api/presign/create-multipart` — initiate multipart upload
- `POST /api/presign/upload-part` — presigned URL for one part
- `POST /api/presign/complete-upload` — assemble multipart object
- `POST /api/presign/confirm` — confirm upload (records true size, enqueues derivatives)
- `POST /api/presign/download` — presigned download URL

## Upload flow

1. `POST /api/presign/upload` (or the multipart trio for files ≥ ~100 MB) to get a
   signed `PUT` URL.
2. `PUT` the bytes **directly to storage** at that URL — not through the API.
3. `POST /api/presign/confirm` to record the real size and enqueue derivative
   generation (thumbnails/posters).

## Idempotency

- Header `Idempotency-Key` (any unique string; UUID recommended).
- Applies to `POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH` (not `GET`).
- A replayed request returns the original response with `idempotency-replayed: true`.
- Cache TTL 24h; scoped per caller + organization + full request URL (query included).

## Rate limits

- 300 requests/minute per organization; an edge limit of ≈600 req/min per IP (Caddy).
- Over limit returns `429 Too Many Requests`:
  `{ "statusCode": 429, "error": "Too Many Requests", "message": "Rate limit exceeded, retry after X seconds" }`
- Redis-backed sliding window (falls back to in-memory if Redis is unavailable).

## Error format

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "<code>",
    "message": "<message>",
    "details": [{ "path": "...", "message": "..." }]
  }
}
```

Status codes: `400` BadRequest/InvalidArgument/ValidationError/InvalidBucketName ·
`401` Unauthorized · `403` Forbidden (valid credential, missing scope/membership) ·
`404` NotFound · `409` Conflict (duplicate name, downgrade blocked by usage) ·
`429` rate limit · `500` InternalError.

---

# 2. MCP Server

Hosted MCP server for AI agents and coding tools. Endpoint
`https://mcp.medialocker.io/mcp`, Streamable HTTP over JSON-RPC, session-aware via the
`Mcp-Session-Id` header (assigned by the server on `initialize`; echo it on every
subsequent request).

## Authentication

Same API keys as the REST API: `Authorization: Bearer <secret-access-key>`. Tool
availability is gated by scope — `read` → read tools, `write` → write tools,
`delete` → destructive tools, `admin` → admin tools (and implies the rest). A
per-credential allowlist can narrow the surface further. The destructive tools
(`delete_object`, `delete_bucket`, `purge`) additionally pass through a firewall that
validates policy and requires confirmation arguments.

Unauthenticated discovery is served at `https://mcp.medialocker.io/llms.txt`,
`.../.well-known/llms.txt`, and `.../mcp/llms.txt`.

## Client configuration

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "medialocker": {
      "url": "https://mcp.medialocker.io/mcp",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer <secret-access-key>"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Tool catalog

**Read tools** (`read` scope)
- `search_media` — full-text search with filters (kind, tags, categories, size, date, bucket)
- `list_buckets` — list buckets with usage summaries
- `get_bucket_info` — bucket details and usage
- `list_objects` — list objects in a bucket (prefix, pagination)
- `get_object_metadata` — object metadata and media-asset info
- `get_object_url` — time-limited presigned download URL
- `list_sets` — list sets in the organization
- `get_usage` — storage usage, egress, request counts
- `get_billing_info` — plan, add-on capacity, billing details

**Write tools** (`write` scope)
- `upload_object` — generate presigned upload instructions
- `create_bucket` — create a bucket (3–63 chars, lowercase, DNS-safe)
- `manage_tags` — create, list, or assign tags
- `manage_categories` — create, list, or assign categories
- `create_set` — create a set for variant renditions
- `add_variant` — add an object variant to a set (aspect ratio + size)
- `generate_variants` — trigger variant generation (enqueues processing jobs)
- `manage_capacity` — add/remove capacity or configure auto-capacity

**Delete tools** (`delete` scope, firewall-gated)
- `delete_object` — soft-delete an object
- `delete_bucket` — delete a bucket (must be empty)
- `purge` — hard-delete all soft-deleted objects in the org; requires `confirm: "DELETE"`

**Admin tools** (`admin` scope)
- `create_api_key` — create a new API key (secret shown once; default 90 days, max 365)

## Resources (read-only context URIs)

- `medialocker://buckets/{id}` — bucket metadata and usage
- `medialocker://media/{id}` — media/object metadata (no binary content)
- `medialocker://usage` — current organization usage summary

## Rate limits

120 requests/minute, 50,000/day per tenant, burst 120. Redis-backed token bucket,
enforced at the gateway before allowlist/audit/handler stages; rejections come back as
JSON-RPC errors. (In production the Redis limiter is mandatory; in-memory fallback is
dev-only via `MCP_ALLOW_MEMORY_RATE_LIMITER=true`.)

---

# 3. CLI

The `medialocker` CLI wraps the REST API and can also run as a local MCP server.

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g medialocker      # or: pnpm add -g medialocker / yarn global add medialocker
npx medialocker --help          # run without installing
```

Binary name `medialocker`; requires Node.js ≥ 22. Source:
`github.com/medialocker/medialocker-cli` (AGPL-3.0).

## Configuration & auth

```bash
medialocker login                 # interactive, or: medialocker login --api-key <secret>
medialocker whoami                # verify auth
medialocker logout
```

- Config file: `~/.config/medialocker/config.json` (honors `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`), written `0600`.
- Env vars: `MEDIALOCKER_API_URL`, `MEDIALOCKER_API_KEY`.
- Config commands: `medialocker config set url <url>`, `config set api-key <secret>`,
  `config get <key>`, `config list`, `config set default-profile <name>`.
- Profiles (multiple instances): `medialocker --profile <name> login --url <url>`, then
  `medialocker --profile <name> <command>`.

## Global flags (any position)

`--json` (machine-readable stdout; logs to stderr) · `--profile <name>` · `--url <url>` ·
`--api-key <key>` · `--no-color` · `--verbose`.

## Command reference

**Buckets:** `buckets list` · `buckets create <name>` · `buckets info <id>` ·
`buckets rm <id> [--yes]`

**Media:** `media list [--bucket --kind --search --tag --category --sort --order --limit --offset]` ·
`media upload <file...> --bucket <id|name> [--key --content-type --tag]` ·
`media download <objectId> --out <path> [--expires <s>]` · `media info <id>` ·
`media rm <id> [--yes]` · `media metadata <id> --set k=v` · `media url <id>` ·
`media thumbnail <id>`

**Search:** `search "<query>"` (same filters as `media list`)

**Tags:** `tags list` · `tags create <name>` · `tags rm <id> [--yes]` ·
`tags set <objectId> --tags <ids>`

**Categories:** `categories list` · `categories create <name> [--parent <id>]` ·
`categories rm <id> [--yes]` · `categories set <objectId> --categories <ids>`

**Sets:** `sets list` · `sets create <name> [--base <objectId>]` · `sets info <id>` ·
`sets add-item <id> --object <objectId>` · `sets rm-item <id> <itemId>` ·
`sets generate <id>` · `sets rm <id> [--yes]`

**Storyboards:** `storyboards list` · `storyboards create <name>` ·
`storyboards info <id>` · `storyboards add-clip <id> --object <objectId>` ·
`storyboards reorder <id> --clips <ids>` · `storyboards update-clip <id> <clipId>` ·
`storyboards rm-clip <id> <clipId>` · `storyboards rm <id> [--yes]`

**Usage:** `usage show` · `usage history --days <n>` · `usage events`

**Billing:** `billing subscription` · `billing portal` · `billing invoices` · `billing plans`

**API keys:** `apikeys list` · `apikeys create <name> [--scopes --bucket --expires-in]` ·
`apikeys revoke <id> [--yes]` · `apikeys rotate <id>`

**MCP:** `mcp serve` (run as a local stdio MCP server) ·
`mcp install --client <claude|cursor> [--write]` (print or merge a client config block)

## Upload & download details

- Single-shot upload for files < 100 MB; automatic multipart for files ≥ 100 MB (parts
  uploaded and completed automatically).
- All transfers use presigned URLs direct to storage (same `presign → PUT → confirm`
  flow as the REST API).
- With `--json`, a successful upload prints
  `{ "objectId", "key", "size", "bucketId", "status": "confirmed" }`.

## Local MCP server (`medialocker mcp serve`)

Exposes a subset of the hosted catalog over stdio: `list_buckets`, `create_bucket`,
`list_media`, `search_media`, `get_object`, `get_object_url`, `upload_object`,
`get_usage`.

## Exit codes

`0` success · `1` generic error · `2` usage error (bad flags/args) · `3` auth error ·
`4` network error · `5` not found.
